hooks.prompt: Check if there are uncommitted code changes by reading the git diff. If there are no changes or only non-code files changed, respond with 'No review needed' and stop. If code was changed, read .github/prompts/code-review.md and review the diff against those guidelines. Focus only on issues CI cannot catch (security, memory leaks, architecture violations, Svelte 5 patterns). Report any findings concisely — one line per issue with file:line and the problem. If everything looks clean, say so in one line.

hooks.command: git status --porcelain | grep -qE '\.(ts|svelte)$' || exit 0; result=$(npm run typecheck 2>&1); [ $? -eq 0 ] && exit 0; jq -n --arg r "$result" '{decision: "block", reason: $r}'

hooks.prompt: You guard an Electron + Svelte 5 app. Check the tool input ($ARGUMENTS) for two things: (1) RENDERER BOUNDARY — if the file is under src/renderer/, deny if it adds runtime imports of Node.js or Electron modules (electron, fs, path, child_process, os, net, http, https, crypto, stream, util, events, or node:-prefixed). 'import type' is fine, only block runtime imports/requires. All Node.js access must go through the preload bridge. (2) IPC NAMING — if the content adds ipcMain.handle, ipcMain.on, ipcRenderer.invoke, or ipcRenderer.send calls, check that channel names follow 'feature:action' convention (e.g. 'shell:execute', 'workspace:list'). Deny if they don't. If no issues found, allow silently.

hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.command' | grep -qiE '\bnpm publish\b|electron-builder.*(--publish|publish)' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Publishing commands are not allowed in Claude Code. Run manually."}}' || true
jq -r '.tool_response.filePath // .tool_input.file_path' | { read -r f; npx prettier --write --ignore-unknown "$f"; } 2>/dev/null || true
